commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared user-turn transcript persistence helper, calls it before CLI/ACP runner execution, and threads a duplicate-suppression flag through post-run transcript persistence and tests.

PR surface: Source +91, Tests +180. Total +271 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The original failure is source-reproducible from current main's post-run-only CLI/ACP persistence, and the PR-introduced internal-session-effects regression is source-reproducible by using sessionEffects: internal without suppressPromptPersistence.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can preserve the contributor's approach while fixing the internal transcript routing regression and adding focused tests.

target.title: fix(agents): persist user turn before attempt failures
target.body:
## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: CLI/ACP turns now persist the accepted user message before the external runner turn can fail, and successful post-run transcript persistence skips the user row when it already exists.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime modules from `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-86592` on macOS, writing a real temporary OpenClaw session JSONL through the production transcript persistence helpers.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF` runtime smoke that imports `persistUserTurnTranscript` and `persistCliTurnTranscript`, writes a visible user request to an actual session transcript, simulates a pre-assistant CLI runner failure, then appends the assistant retry with `userAlreadyPersisted: true`.
Evidence after fix:

```text
runnerFailureAfterUserPersisted=simulated CLI runner failed before assistant reply
afterFailureMessages=["user:visible user request"]
afterRetrySuccessRoles=["user","assistant"]
userMessageCount=1
```

I also ran the targeted regression suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`, which completed with 5 passed test files and 278 passed tests.
Observed result after fix: After the simulated failed runner attempt, the real session transcript still contains the accepted user request. After a later successful CLI transcript append, the transcript contains exactly one user row followed by one assistant row, proving the duplicate-prevention flag keeps retry/success persistence idempotent.
What was not tested: I did not launch a live external Claude/Codex CLI subprocess or a live ACP backend; this was a local OpenClaw runtime transcript smoke using real session persistence code, supplemented by targeted regression tests.

## Summary
- add a shared user-only transcript persistence helper
- persist ACP and CLI user turns before the runner can throw
- skip duplicate user-row mirroring when the current turn was already persisted

## Tests
- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF` local OpenClaw runtime transcript smoke
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

Fixes #86592
